messages.author: fiorex
messages.text: hello @Habbie i just trying to do the same think, use meshcore inside EspHome firmware, i also opened a github issue https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/1225 did you mind share you experience and code? Thanks!

embeds_html:
Integration with EspHome · Issue #1225 · meshcore-dev/MeshCore
Hello everyone, I’ve just discovered this beautiful open-source project because I want to build a private LoRa mesh for a hobby project. Specifically, I’m using ESPHome as the base firmware for som...

Renze Nicolai (@renze@hsnl.social)
-# ↩ Peter van Dijk @Habbie yes, it is meant as a library but it is in early stages of development. License is MIT. PRs and issues are of course more than welcome! I am going to use this library for Tanmatsu, an esp32-p4 based handheld terminal with LoRa radio.
HSNL Social • 2025-12-15 2:33 p.m.

Packet Transport Component
Instructions for setting up a packet transport component on ESPHome

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: so. a meshcore component should be usable as an esphome 'packet transport'
messages.content_html: so. a meshcore component should be usable as an esphome 'packet transport'

messages.author: fiorex
messages.text: every node will have sensors and could be connected to Internet via WiFi so i think i do not need any repeaters. didn't know about Packet Transport Component, but reading documentation, i don't understand if i need to know in adavance all the devices that will be "connected", but i want a "self-healing" mesh where when i turn on a new LoRa enabled node it will automatically part of the mesh
messages.content_html: every node will have sensors and could be connected to Internet via WiFi so i think i do not need any repeaters. didn't know about Packet Transport Component, but reading documentation, i don't understand if i need to know in adavance all the devices that will be "connected", but i want a "self-healing" mesh where when i turn on a new LoRa enabled node it will automatically part of the mesh

messages.author: fiorex
messages.text: @Habbie what do you think?
messages.content_html: @Habbie what do you think?

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: I don't follow why you want LoRa if every node can have wifi
messages.content_html: I don't follow why you want LoRa if every node can have wifi

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: i'm thinking about your auto mesh thing. If it's meshcore, routing is a sorted problem, as long as repeaters (if necessary) are present. For discovery, maybe all devices should send their messages to the same channel, that you then pick up in one place. But I don't yet know how autodiscovery would fit into esphome.
messages.content_html: i'm thinking about your auto mesh thing. If it's meshcore, routing is a sorted problem, as long as repeaters (if necessary) are present. For discovery, maybe all devices should send their messages to the same channel, that you then pick up in one place. But I don't yet know how autodiscovery would fit into esphome.

messages.author: fiorex
messages.text: maybe i didn't explain well, sorry, what i wanted to say is that it could be that some nodes have wifi, but not all nodes will be reached by wifi so i need LoRa for this. maybe i didn't understand correctly but meshcore is already a self-healing mesh right? when a new device turn on it will send an "hello" packet right?
messages.content_html: maybe i didn't explain well, sorry, what i wanted to say is that it could be that some nodes have wifi, but not all nodes will be reached by wifi so i need LoRa for this. maybe i didn't understand correctly but meshcore is already a self-healing mesh right? when a new device turn on it will send an "hello" packet right?
messages.reply_html:
Habbie
I don't follow why you want LoRa if every node can have wifi

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: devices do announce themselves periodically, but if we put esphome on them to send out metrics, the sending out itself will show the existence of that device I suppose
messages.content_html: devices do announce themselves periodically, but if we put esphome on them to send out metrics, the sending out itself will show the existence of that device I suppose

messages.author: fiorex
messages.text: one thing is not 100% clear to me: can i use meshcore for a private mesh network? and can i send a LoRa message directly to a specific node? following my previous example if a node X with wifi receive an mqtt message for node Y, can the node X send a LoRa message with destination Y, even if there is N hope beetween X and Y?
messages.content_html: one thing is not 100% clear to me: can i use meshcore for a private mesh network? and can i send a LoRa message directly to a specific node? following my previous example if a node X with wifi receive an mqtt message for node Y, can the node X send a LoRa message with destination Y, even if there is N hope beetween X and Y?

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: a lora message is just a bunch of bits in the air. those bits might be a meshcore message, in which case meshcore devices might handle the bits, and repeat them
messages.content_html: a lora message is just a bunch of bits in the air. those bits might be a meshcore message, in which case meshcore devices might handle the bits, and repeat them

messages.author: fiorex
messages.text: ok, what i thinked is correct than with meshcore i can say "send this message to this node_id" and it will take care of routing and repeating.
messages.content_html: ok, what i thinked is correct than with meshcore i can say "send this message to this node_id" and it will take care of routing and repeating.

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: as i understand it, if one esphome has wifi, and it can talk to another one over Packet Transport, the first one can relay state to HA
messages.content_html: as i understand it, if one esphome has wifi, and it can talk to another one over Packet Transport, the first one can relay state to HA

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: oh right, reading another comment on the github ticket from you. yes, then the decoder might be enough with a bit of lambda glue in esphome
messages.content_html: oh right, reading another comment on the github ticket from you. yes, then the decoder might be enough with a bit of lambda glue in esphome

messages.author: fiorex
messages.text: yes, i think you're right. Did you do some tests with porting meshcore-c to esphome? did you mind sharing your work? thanks!
messages.content_html: yes, i think you're right. Did you do some tests with porting meshcore-c to esphome? did you mind sharing your work? thanks!
